description: Dev-tool cache & build-artifact cleanup catalog. Drives the Dev Cleaner sub-tab in mChatAI+ System | Storage. Each category is a row in the optimizer UI; the Swift binary is a generic scanner/executor and never hardcodes paths or commands.

ui.emptyStateTitle: Pick a category from the left.
ui.emptyStateHelp: Every category, command, and verdict comes from mchatai-source/wisdom/packs/storage-optimizer.json — adding new tools is a JSON edit, no rebuild.
ui.uiOnlyLabel: Opens external app — no in-app cleanup.

tccBanner.title: mChatAIShell isn't running — scans limited to /Applications
tccBanner.body: mChatAI+ runs sandboxed, so it can't read ~/Library directly. The unsandboxed companion app mChatAIShell does all the dev-tool scans (du, simctl, rm) on its behalf. Launch mChatAIShell and the sizes will populate immediately.
tccBanner.afterGrantInstructions: If mChatAIShell is already running, check Settings → Shell Bridge that the port/token are configured. Click Rescan after launch.

categories.name: Xcode DerivedData
categories.verdict: Always regenerates on next build.
categories.why: Per-project incremental build caches. Cold rebuild ~3-5 minutes per project.

smartVerdict.rule: Show entries whose last-modified date is >14 days as STALE.

categories.name: iOS DeviceSupport (real-device symbols)
categories.verdict: Delete iOS versions you no longer test on physical devices.
categories.why: Debug symbols for each iOS version connected via USB. Re-downloads on next connect.

categories.name: iOS Simulator runtimes (devices)
categories.verdict: Delete entire runtimes you're not actively testing against; each device is ~5-11 GB.
categories.why: Every simulator runtime carries an OS image + per-device data. Mass-purge unused runtimes.
categories.postCommandReason: After a runtime is deleted, its per-device data at ~/Library/Developer/CoreSimulator/Devices/<UDID>/ becomes orphaned (~3-10 GB per device). `simctl delete unavailable` walks all device dirs and removes those whose runtime is no longer installed — that's where the real reclaim happens.
categories.trashableReason: simctl manages its own deletion atomically; rm -rf on the dir would orphan CoreSimulatorService state. NOTE: xcrun simctl runtime delete requires the BUILDVERSION (e.g. 21C62) — the SimRuntime identifier silently fails. Multiple runtimes can share an identifier (iOS 17.0 + 17.0.1 both 'iOS-17-0').

smartVerdict.rule: Detect lowest iOS deployment target across user repos and mark older runtimes as SAFE.

categories.name: SwiftPM clone cache
categories.verdict: Always safe. Re-clones on next swift build.
categories.why: Swift Package Manager keeps a global clone cache of package git repos.

categories.name: Android NDK versions
categories.verdict: Keep latest only unless gradle pins ndkVersion in any repo.
categories.why: Android Native Development Kit — the C/C++ toolchain (clang, libc++, sysroots) used to compile native modules in Android apps. React Native / Expo / Flutter auto-pick the latest installed version unless a project's build.gradle pins ndkVersion. Each version is 2-3 GB. Path: ~/Library/Android/sdk/ndk/<version>/. Items below are version numbers; the highest is what your projects use.

smartVerdict.rule: Search repos for ndkVersion pin; mark unreferenced versions SAFE.

categories.name: Android emulator system-images
categories.verdict: Delete API levels you don't emulate against.
categories.why: Each system-image is ~3-4 GB. Only the API level your emulators boot to is needed.

categories.name: Android AVDs (Virtual Devices)
categories.verdict: Delete AVDs you no longer launch.
categories.why: Each AVD is 2-7 GB of disk image + snapshots.

categories.name: Gradle caches
categories.verdict: Always safe. Repopulates on next gradle build.
categories.why: Dependency jars, transforms, and build outputs.

categories.name: Yarn cache
categories.verdict: Always safe.
categories.why: Global yarn package cache.
categories.trashableReason: yarn cache clean handles structure properly; raw rm leaves a stale .yarnrc state.

categories.name: npm cache
categories.verdict: Always safe.
categories.why: Global npm package cache.

categories.name: pnpm store
categories.verdict: Safe if no active pnpm workspaces.
categories.why: Global content-addressed pnpm package store.

categories.name: Playwright browsers
categories.verdict: Delete only if you don't run Playwright validators.
categories.why: Bundled chromium/webkit/firefox builds for E2E testing.

categories.name: Repo node_modules (workspace scan)
categories.verdict: Always safe. Reinstall when needed.
categories.why: Per-repo JavaScript deps installed by npm/yarn/pnpm. mChatAI scans ~/mystuff/src up to 4 levels deep; each row below is one repo's node_modules. Reinstalling takes ~1-5 min per repo (cached via npm/yarn). Most disposable category in Dev Cleaner.

categories.name: Repo SwiftPM .build dirs
categories.verdict: Always safe. Regenerates on next swift build.
categories.why: Per-package SwiftPM build artifacts (compiled .o files, dependency clones, generated headers). Created when you run `swift build` or open a Swift Package in Xcode. mChatAI scans ~/mystuff/src up to 5 levels deep for any .build dir. Each rebuild is a fresh cold compile (~30s-5min depending on package). Safe to nuke any time.

categories.name: Docker Desktop VM
categories.verdict: Use Docker UI → Troubleshoot → Clean / Purge data.
categories.why: Docker VM disk image. Direct rm can corrupt Docker Desktop state — always go through the app.

categories.name: HuggingFace model cache
categories.verdict: Models re-download on next use. Keep ones you use weekly.
categories.why: Downloaded transformer models, embeddings, datasets.

categories.name: uv (Python) cache
categories.verdict: Always safe.
categories.why: uv package manager wheel cache.

categories.name: pip cache
categories.verdict: Always safe.
categories.why: pip wheel cache.

categories.name: Unity Editor versions
categories.verdict: Each Unity Editor install is ~10-15 GB. Keep only the version your active projects use.
categories.why: Use Unity Hub → Installs to uninstall properly.

categories.name: Homebrew cache
categories.verdict: Always safe.
categories.why: Downloaded bottle tarballs.

categories.name: Chrome cache
categories.verdict: Always safe. Browser will repopulate on use.
categories.why: Chrome cached web resources.

categories.name: mChatAI app caches (internal)
categories.verdict: Per-session staging dirs. Always safe to wipe after sessions complete.
categories.why: Per-DevSession workspace staging dirs accumulate after wizard runs.
